Anmol alias Pinky, who is being described by investigators as one of Pakistanâs biggest cocaine dealers, was arrested during a major anti-narcotics operation in Karachi. According to police and media reports, she allegedly operated a large drug supply network involved in cocaine, ice (crystal meth), ecstasy, and party pills across upscale areas of the city. Authorities say she had been wanted by multiple law enforcement agencies for several years.

Initial investigations suggest that the network used online orders, delivery riders, and branded packaging to distribute narcotics worth millions of rupees. Officials also claimed the operation had links to educational institutions, where drugs were allegedly supplied to students in colleges and universities. During the raid, police reportedly recovered cocaine, chemicals, weapons, and other narcotics from her possession.

After her arrest, Anmol alias Pinky was presented before the court in Karachi as investigations continued. Reports say law enforcement agencies expect major revelations from her interrogation that could expose the wider drug network and its associates. The case has gained nationwide attention due to the scale of the alleged operation and its reported reach into affluent neighborhoods and educational institutions.
